sections.title: Executive Summary
sections.content:
AMD closes 2025-12-27 with $34.6B of revenue (34.3% YoY) and $4.33B of net income (164.2% YoY). The period ended with an operating margin of 10.7% and a net margin of 12.5%, which frames how much of the reported scale translated into retained profitability. Revenue and earnings improved together, indicating that the growth story carried through to reported profitability.

Operating cash flow reached $7.71B (153.5% YoY), and after $974M of capital spending the business produced $6.74B of free cash flow (180.0% YoY). Cash generation exceeded reported earnings, a sign that the period converted accounting profit into liquidity with relatively little friction. Capital spending absorbed 2.8% of revenue, which helps frame how much internal cash flow remained available after reinvestment.

Liquidity and leverage are both relatively manageable, with a current ratio of 2.85x and debt-to-equity of 0.05x. The quick ratio of 2.01x sits below the current ratio, indicating that inventory or other less-liquid current assets carry part of the coverage burden.

sections.title: Trend Analysis
sections.content:
Across the annual periods shown here, revenue provides the clearest recurring pattern, with the latest value at $34.6B and a compound annual growth rate of 10.4%. The multi-year trend is positive, but the pace is moderate rather than transformative.

Cash generation remained constructive, with $7.71B of operating cash flow translating into $6.74B of free cash flow. Balance-sheet pressure should be read through both leverage (0.05x debt-to-equity) and liquidity (2.85x current ratio), because those measures do not always point to the same conclusion about financial flexibility.

sections.title: Risk Factors
sections.content: No single balance-sheet or earnings issue dominates the reporting period used in this note. The main areas to monitor remain profitability at 10.7% and free cash flow at $6.74B, because those lines usually determine whether the balance sheet is strengthening or merely holding steady.

sections.title: Analyst Notes
sections.content:
The most constructive part of AMD's profile is straightforward: Revenue rose 34.3% year over year, marking a meaningful step-up in scale. Current ratio of 2.85x provides a wide cushion against near-term obligations. That matters because leverage (0.05x debt-to-equity), liquidity (2.85x current ratio), and free cash flow ($6.74B) need to be read together rather than in isolation.

This note is anchored to the annual period ending 2025-12-27, with the prior period set at 2024-12-28. Metrics without the required statement support remain blank rather than estimated, so the analysis stays tied to the filing evidence rather than to gap-filled assumptions.

sections.title: Executive Summary
sections.rendered_content:
AMD closes 2025-12-27 with $34.6B of revenue (34.3% YoY) and $4.33B of net income (164.2% YoY). The period ended with an operating margin of 10.7% and a net margin of 12.5%, which frames how much of the reported scale translated into retained profitability. Revenue and earnings improved together, indicating that the growth story carried through to reported profitability.

Operating cash flow reached $7.71B (153.5% YoY), and after $974M of capital spending the business produced $6.74B of free cash flow (180.0% YoY). Cash generation exceeded reported earnings, a sign that the period converted accounting profit into liquidity with relatively little friction. Capital spending absorbed 2.8% of revenue, which helps frame how much internal cash flow remained available after reinvestment.

Liquidity and leverage are both relatively manageable, with a current ratio of 2.85x and debt-to-equity of 0.05x. The quick ratio of 2.01x sits below the current ratio, indicating that inventory or other less-liquid current assets carry part of the coverage burden.

sections.title: Trend Analysis
sections.rendered_content:
Across the annual periods shown here, revenue provides the clearest recurring pattern, with the latest value at $34.6B and a compound annual growth rate of 10.4%. The multi-year trend is positive, but the pace is moderate rather than transformative.

Cash generation remained constructive, with $7.71B of operating cash flow translating into $6.74B of free cash flow. Balance-sheet pressure should be read through both leverage (0.05x debt-to-equity) and liquidity (2.85x current ratio), because those measures do not always point to the same conclusion about financial flexibility.

sections.title: Analyst Notes
sections.rendered_content:
The most constructive part of AMD's profile is straightforward: Revenue rose 34.3% year over year, marking a meaningful step-up in scale. Current ratio of 2.85x provides a wide cushion against near-term obligations. That matters because leverage (0.05x debt-to-equity), liquidity (2.85x current ratio), and free cash flow ($6.74B) need to be read together rather than in isolation.

This note is anchored to the annual period ending 2025-12-27, with the prior period set at 2024-12-28. Metrics without the required statement support remain blank rather than estimated, so the analysis stays tied to the filing evidence rather than to gap-filled assumptions.
